# list-toolkit

> Zero-dependency JavaScript library providing efficient list-based data structures: linked lists, caches, heaps, queues, stacks, splay trees, and skip lists.

- NPM: https://npmjs.org/package/list-toolkit
- GitHub: https://github.com/uhop/list-toolkit
- Wiki: https://github.com/uhop/list-toolkit/wiki
- License: BSD-3-Clause

## Install

```bash
npm install list-toolkit
```

## Quick start

```js
import ValueList from 'list-toolkit/value-list.js';

const list = ValueList.from([1, 2, 3]);
for (const value of list) console.log(value); // 1, 2, 3

list.pushBack(4);
list.pushFront(0);
console.log(list.popFront()); // 0
```

```js
import List from 'list-toolkit/list.js';

// Node-based list: link properties are added directly to your objects
const a = {name: 'Alice'}, b = {name: 'Bob'};
const people = List.from([a, b]);
for (const node of people) console.log(node.name); // Alice, Bob
```

## Modules

### Linked lists (doubly linked, circular)

- **List** (`list-toolkit/list.js`) ‚Äî hosted node-based DLL. Link properties (`next`/`prev`) are added to user objects. Customizable link names.
- **ValueList** (`list-toolkit/value-list.js`) ‚Äî hosted value-based DLL. Wraps values in `ValueNode` containers.
- **ExtList** (`list-toolkit/ext-list.js`) ‚Äî external (headless) node-based DLL. Points into an existing circular list.
- **ExtValueList** (`list-toolkit/ext-value-list.js`) ‚Äî external (headless) value-based DLL.

### Linked lists (singly linked, circular)

- **SList** (`list-toolkit/slist.js`) ‚Äî hosted node-based SLL.
- **ValueSList** (`list-toolkit/value-slist.js`) ‚Äî hosted value-based SLL.
- **ExtSList** (`list-toolkit/ext-slist.js`) ‚Äî external (headless) node-based SLL.
- **ExtValueSList** (`list-toolkit/ext-value-slist.js`) ‚Äî external (headless) value-based SLL.

### Unrolled list

- **UnrolledList** (`list-toolkit/unrolled-list.js`) ‚Äî chunked value list for bulk pipelines: one allocation per `chunkSize` (default 64) values, contiguous iteration, no growth copies. Deque-style API (`pushFront`/`pushBack`/`popFront`/`popBack` + `push`/`pop`/`shift`/`unshift` aliases), O(1) tracked `size`, `at(index)`. Front/back ops and iteration only.

### Caches

- **CacheLRU** (`list-toolkit/cache.js`) ‚Äî least recently used eviction. Default export.
- **CacheFIFO** (`list-toolkit/cache/cache-fifo.js`) ‚Äî first in first out eviction.
- **CacheLFU** (`list-toolkit/cache/cache-lfu.js`) ‚Äî least frequently used eviction.
- **CacheRandom** (`list-toolkit/cache/cache-random.js`) ‚Äî random eviction.
- **CacheSLRU** (`list-toolkit/cache/cache-slru.js`) ‚Äî segmented LRU: scan-resistant (hits promote into a protected segment).
- **CacheClock** (`list-toolkit/cache/cache-clock.js`) ‚Äî CLOCK (second chance): reads only set a bit; approximates LRU at FIFO cost.
- **cacheDecorator** (`list-toolkit/cache.js`) ‚Äî decorator to cache function/method results.

All caches share the same API: `has(key)`, `find(key)`/`get(key)`, `peek(key)` (no side effects), `register(key, value)`/`set(key, value)`, `remove(key)`/`delete(key)`, `evict()`, `setCapacity(n)`, `clear()`.

### Heaps (priority queues)

- **MinHeap** (`list-toolkit/heap.js`) ‚Äî array-based binary min-heap. Default export.
- **IndexedHeap** (`list-toolkit/heap/indexed-heap.js`) ‚Äî min-heap with intrusive element indices (configurable property name): O(1) `has`/`findIndex`, O(log n) `update`/`remove` by handle (decrease-key). Elements must be objects.
- **LeftistHeap** (`list-toolkit/heap/leftist-heap.js`) ‚Äî merge-based leftist heap.
- **SkewHeap** (`list-toolkit/heap/skew-heap.js`) ‚Äî merge-based skew heap.
- **PairingHeap** (`list-toolkit/heap/pairing-heap.js`) ‚Äî merge-based pairing heap: O(1) `push`/`merge`; `push` returns a node handle; `update(node, isDecreased?)` (decrease-key) and `remove(node)` by handle.

All heaps support `less` or `compare` for ordering. Common API: `push(value)`, `pop()`, `top`/`peek()`, `merge()`, `has(value)`, `remove(value)`.

### Adapters

- **Queue** (`list-toolkit/queue.js`) ‚Äî FIFO queue adapter. Methods: `add`/`push`/`enqueue`, `remove`/`pop`/`dequeue`, `peek`, `top`, `clear`.
- **Stack** (`list-toolkit/stack.js`) ‚Äî LIFO stack adapter. Methods: `push`, `pop`, `peek`, `top`, `clear`.
- **Deque** (`list-toolkit/deque.js`) ‚Äî double-ended queue adapter. Methods: `pushFront`/`unshift`, `pushBack`/`push`, `popFront`/`shift`, `popBack`/`pop`, `peekFront`/`peekBack`, `rotate(n)`, `clear`. O(1) at both ends.
- **RingBuffer** (`list-toolkit/ring-buffer.js`) ‚Äî array-backed deque on a circular buffer, same API as Deque plus `at(index)` (O(1) random access) and optional `{capacity}` bounded keep-last-N mode. Fastest queue/deque for raw throughput.

### Trees

- **SplayTree** (`list-toolkit/tree/splay-tree.js`) ‚Äî self-adjusting BST. Methods: `insert`, `find`, `remove`, `promote`, `split` (via `splitMaxTree`), `join`.

### Skip list

- **SkipList** (`list-toolkit/skip-list.js`) ‚Äî probabilistic ordered container, expected O(log n) operations, reads don't mutate. Methods: `insert`, `find`, `has`, `remove`, `floor`, `ceil`, `getMin`/`getMax`, `popFront`, ordered/reverse/range iteration.

### Timer wheel

- **TimerWheel** (`list-toolkit/timer-wheel.js`) ‚Äî hashed timing wheel, logical time (caller drives the clock). Methods: `schedule(value, delay)` ‚Üí handle, `cancel(handle)`, `reschedule(handle, delay)`, `isScheduled`, `remainingTicks`, `tick()` ‚Üí due values, `advance(n)`, `clear`. All scheduling ops O(1).

### Free list

- **FreeList** (`list-toolkit/free-list.js`) ‚Äî intrusive object pool: `acquire()`/`release(node)` recycle nodes through their own link property; `reset` hook scrubs payloads; optional `capacity`. A GC-pressure tool, not a throughput optimization.

### Utilities

- **list-utils** (`list-toolkit/list-utils.js`) ‚Äî push/append values, find/remove nodes by condition, validation.
- **nt-utils** (`list-toolkit/nt-utils.js`) ‚Äî convert between null-terminated and circular lists.
- **meta-utils** (`list-toolkit/meta-utils.js`) ‚Äî `addAlias`, `addAliases`, iterator helpers, `copyOptions`.

## Key concepts

- All lists are **circular** ‚Äî the last node links back to the head.
- **Hosted lists** use a `HeadNode` sentinel. **External lists** are headless.
- **Value lists** wrap values in `ValueNode`. **Node lists** use link properties directly on user objects.
- Link names (`nextName`/`prevName`) are customizable, allowing objects to be in multiple lists.
- **Pointers** (`Ptr`) provide safe iteration with insert/remove during traversal.
- **TypeScript** declarations (`.d.ts`) are included for all modules.

## Links

- Docs: https://github.com/uhop/list-toolkit/wiki
- Choosing a structure (selection guide): https://github.com/uhop/list-toolkit/wiki/Choosing-a-data-structure
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/list-toolkit
- Full LLM reference: https://github.com/uhop/list-toolkit/blob/master/llms-full.txt
